planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: The smallest planet and closest to the Sun. Has no atmosphere and extreme temperature swings between day and night.

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: The hottest planet, wrapped in a thick toxic atmosphere of carbon dioxide. Rotates backwards relative to most planets.

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: Our home planet — the only known world to harbor life, with liquid water oceans and a protective atmosphere.

planets.name: Mars
planets.description: The Red Planet, home to the Solar System's largest volcano (Olympus Mons) and deepest canyon (Valles Marineris).

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: The largest planet. Its Great Red Spot is a storm bigger than Earth that has raged for centuries.

planets.name: Saturn
planets.description: Famous for its stunning rings of ice and rock. Less dense than water — it would float in a large enough bathtub.

planets.name: Uranus
planets.description: Rotates on its side, giving it extreme 42-year-long seasons. Blue-green color comes from methane in its atmosphere.

planets.name: Neptune
planets.description: The windiest planet in the Solar System, with supersonic gales up to 2,100 km/h. Deep blue from atmospheric methane.

moons.name: Moon
moons.description: Earth's only natural satellite and the fifth-largest moon in the Solar System.

moons.name: Phobos
moons.description: The larger of Mars' two tiny moons. It orbits so close that it is slowly spiraling inward.

moons.name: Deimos
moons.description: The smaller, outermost moon of Mars. Likely a captured asteroid.

moons.name: Io
moons.description: The most volcanically active body in the Solar System, with over 400 active volcanoes.

moons.name: Europa
moons.description: An icy shell covers a global subsurface ocean — a prime candidate in the search for extraterrestrial life.

moons.name: Ganymede
moons.description: The largest moon in the Solar System — bigger than Mercury — and the only moon known to generate its own magnetic field.

moons.name: Callisto
moons.description: The most heavily cratered object in the Solar System, with a surface that has barely changed in billions of years.

moons.name: Titan
moons.description: The only moon with a thick atmosphere and stable surface liquids — lakes and rivers of liquid methane.

moons.name: Enceladus
moons.description: Shoots plumes of water-ice geysers into space from an ocean beneath its icy crust.

moons.name: Mimas
moons.description: Sometimes called the Death Star moon because of the massive Herschel crater on one side.

moons.name: Rhea
moons.description: Saturn's second-largest moon. There is tentative evidence of a thin ring system of its own.

moons.name: Triton
moons.description: The only large moon with a retrograde orbit — probably a captured Kuiper Belt object.

moons.name: Miranda
moons.description: Has some of the Solar System's most extreme terrain, including 20km-high cliffs.

moons.name: Ariel
moons.description: The brightest and possibly youngest surface among Uranus' major moons.

moons.name: Titania
moons.description: The largest moon of Uranus. Huge canyons and fault lines scar its frozen surface.
